title: Seahorse Assay for Measuring Oxygen Consumption Rate in iPSC-Derived Neurons
author: Tzu-Chieh Huang

steps.instruction: Seed 1.65x10^5^ Day 5 iPSC-derived glutamatergic neurons (iGLUTs) into each well of a XF24 microplate and continue differentiation until Day 21 for the mitochondrial stress test.
Prior to the assay, remove most of the culture medium, leaving only 50 ¬ĶL per well, and add 1000 ¬ĶL pre-warmed Seahorse XF DMEM supplemented with 25mM glucose and 0.23mM pyruvate.
Incubate the Seahorse plate at 37 ¬įC in a non-CO2 incubator for 1 hour.
After the 1-hour incubation, replace the assay medium with 500 ¬ĶL of fresh assay medium for the Seahorse assay.

Measure the plate using Seahorse XFe24 Analyzer with the standard Mito Stress Test program. The program includes three sequential injections of mitochondrial inhibitors: 1.5 ¬ĶM oligomycin, 1.5 ¬ĶM carbonyl cyanide 4-(trifluoromethoxy) phenylhydrazone, and a mixture of 0.5 ¬ĶM rotenone and 0.5 ¬ĶM antimycin A.

steps.instruction: After recording the oxygen consumption rate, lyse the cells with 30 ¬ĶL of M-PER‚ĄĘ Mammalian Protein Extraction Reagent containing cOmplete‚ĄĘ Mini Protease Inhibitor Cocktail and PhosSTOP‚ĄĘ, according to the manufacturer‚Äôs instructions.
Measure total protein concentration using Pierce‚ĄĘ Dilution-Free‚ĄĘ Rapid Gold BCA Protein Assay.
Normalize oxygen consumption rates to total protein content.
